ground_truth.title: Learn graphic design online: courses & degrees | edX
ground_truth.main_content:
Shape your career in graphic design with online courses and programs. Gain creative problem-solving and communication skills while mastering design software used across industries.

Graphic design is the digital fusion of art and advertising. It's everywhere — from the signs guiding us through forest trails to the websites we browse and the logos corporations use. Graphic designers combine text and imagery to create visuals that convey messages, guide behavior, and promote products. These professionals have a keen eye for aesthetics, strong attention to detail, a knack for storytelling, and proficiency with design software.

Learning graphic design online can help you become a graphic designer, perfect your personal branding, bring creative ideas to life, and learn valuable software skills like Adobe InDesign, Adobe Illustrator, and Figma.

In graphic design courses and programs, you can expect to learn the basics of color theory, visual communication and storytelling, understanding client needs and target audience, typography fundamentals, accessibility in design, design thinking, data visualization, delivering visual presentations, user experience and user interface design, and human-centered design techniques.

edX offers graphic design courses that are short, flexible, self-paced programs lasting 2–6 weeks. Professional certificate programs help build career-ready skills in 2–10 months and earn verified certificates from leading universities. Executive education programs for professionals last 6–8 weeks. Online degree programs range from 2–8 years and can provide a foundation in design principles and a professional portfolio.

Graphic designers create brand identities, enhance customer experience, and communicate ideas visually in technology, education, fashion, healthcare, publishing, retail, and entertainment industries. Specializations include branding, advertising, illustration, UI and UX design, experiential design, motion graphics and animation, packaging design, publication and typographic design, and web design.

Career options with graphic design skills include graphic designer with a median annual salary of $61,300, art director with a median annual salary of $111,040, digital designer with a median annual salary of $95,380, and desktop publisher with a median annual salary of $53,620.
